body_txt: Every opponent, no matter how strong, has their weaknesses. Some people ready countermeasures to exploit those weaknesses. Others become so strong that they can simply overpower their enemy without needing such preparation. And sometimes, these two approaches are combined to ensure that the opponent stands little or any chance of survival. In short, you've got a weapon (potentially even a living, breathing individual ) built for the sole purpose of putting someone down. Sometimes, for an aspiring villain, The Hero can be too much for mere Mooks to kill. Maybe they're dealing with a cadre of powerful heroes they need to get rid of, each with colorful and fantastical abilities. Nothing is without weakness, but having multiple enemies can make dealing with all those vulnerabilities at once difficult to do by your lonesome. Such is where examples of this trope come in, to simplify matters by being wholly made to erase the enemy. An example of this trope could become a Hero Killer , eliminating an important target through their outpacing of the others' abilities. May overlap with Elite Mook , if a villain's minion is given this role, and The Psycho Rangers if done so with multiple individuals. To overcome such an example, a hero would need to set themselves up to act beyond what this adversary may know. Confusion Fu may come into play to deceive them by acting contrary to one's usual nature. Eleventh Hour Superpowers could become a factor, to shock the enemy with an advantage neither the hero nor villain may have expected. Alternatively, if a hero resorts to this trope, they may employ the use of a Villain-Beating Artifact . Compare with Adaptive Ability , in which a character inherently changes their powers to counter new enemies. Compare and contrast with Crazy-Prepared , in which a character readies themselves for whatever their enemy may throw at them, even if they're weaker than them, and Always Someone Better , in which a character simply outdoes all their opponent can do, perhaps without even needing to be aware of them. Also compare with Single-Target Law , in which a law, rule, or restriction is set up targeting a designated individual. For a more comedic fix, compare Counter Zany , where a crazy plan is met in kind with an even crazier plan. May potentially result in Crippling Overspecialization , since being super-oriented to fight a single threat can make one very vulnerable to all others. Related to Highly Specific Counterplay in which a super-specific counter (not necessarily a lethal one) is made to one thing, but proves to be useless against others. Super-Trope to Laser-Guided Tyke-Bomb and Weapon of X-Slaying .
